entries.definition:
them [the plants] and they gain thickness, and rise straight upon their stalks.
غَلِيظ ghalīẓ [quasi-act. part.] 1 harsh, severe (31:24) نُمَتِّعُهُمْ قَلِيلًا ثُمَّ نَضْطَرُّهُمْ إِلَى عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ We let them enjoy themselves for a while, then We drive them into a severe chastisement 2 callous, heartless, inhuman (3:159) وَلَوْ كُنْتَ فَظًّا غَلِيظَ الْقَلْبِ لَانْفَضُّوا مِنْ حَوْلِكَ had you been foul-mouthed, hard-hearted, they would have dispersed from about you 3 solemn, formidable, firm (4:154) وَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُمْ مِيثَاقًا غَلِيظًا and We took from them a solemn pledge.

غِلَاظ ghilāẓ [pl. n.] mighty, strong ones, firm, stern (66:6) عَلَيْهَا مَلَائِكَةٌ غِلَاظٌ شِدَادٌ and over it [Hell] are angels, stern and strong.

غِلْظَة ghilẓatun [n./v. n.] severity, harshness (9:123) وَلْيَجِدُوا فِيكُمْ غِلْظَةً and let them find harshness in you.
entries.definition_english:
them [the plants] and they gain thickness, and rise straight upon their stalks.
غَلِيظ ghalīẓ [quasi-act. part.] 1 harsh, severe (31:24) نُمَتِّعُهُمْ قَلِيلًا ثُمَّ نَضْطَرُّهُمْ إِلَى عَذَابٍ غَلِيظٍ We let them enjoy themselves for a while, then We drive them into a severe chastisement 2 callous, heartless, inhuman (3:159) وَلَوْ كُنْتَ فَظًّا غَلِيظَ الْقَلْبِ لَانْفَضُّوا مِنْ حَوْلِكَ had you been foul-mouthed, hard-hearted, they would have dispersed from about you 3 solemn, formidable, firm (4:154) وَأَخَذْنَا مِنْهُمْ مِيثَاقًا غَلِيظًا and We took from them a solemn pledge.

غِلَاظ ghilāẓ [pl. n.] mighty, strong ones, firm, stern (66:6) عَلَيْهَا مَلَائِكَةٌ غِلَاظٌ شِدَادٌ and over it [Hell] are angels, stern and strong.

غِلْظَة ghilẓatun [n./v. n.] severity, harshness (9:123) وَلْيَجِدُوا فِيكُمْ غِلْظَةً and let them find harshness in you.

entries.definition:
gh–l–l extreme thirst; to bar from drinking; shackles, to shackle; treachery, animosity, hatred; to confiscate; to purloin, to yield, to produce, to earn. Of this root, five forms occur 16 times in the Qurʾan: غَلَّ ghalla four times; غُلَّت ghullat once; غِلّ ghill three times; الْأَغْلَال al-aghlāl six times and مَغْلُولَة maghlūlatun twice.
غَلَّ ghalla u I [v. intrans.] to defraud, to purloin (3:161) وَمَا كَانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَنْ يَغُلَّ it is inconceivable that a Prophet would ever purloin

674

gh–l–l extreme thirst; to bar from drinking; shackles, to shackle; treachery, animosity, hatred; to confiscate; to purloin, to yield, to produce, to earn. Of this root, five forms occur 16 times in the Qurʾan: غَلَّ ghalla four times; غُلَّت ghullat once; غِلّ ghill three times; الْأَغْلَال al-aghlāl six times and مَغْلُولَة maghlūlatun twice.
غَلَّ ghalla u I [v. intrans.] to defraud, to purloin (3:161) وَمَا كَانَ لِنَبِيٍّ أَنْ يَغُلَّ it is inconceivable that a Prophet would ever purloin
